sections.paragraph: (“pertaining to a hill,” i.e. built on a hill), Gibeon, a great town of the Hivites ( Jos 10:2 11:19 ), afterwards of the Benjamites ( Jos 18:25 21:17 ), to be distinguished from the neighbouring towns Geba (גֶּבַע) and Gibeah (גִּבְעָה), and situated to the north [or rather west] of both. In the reigns of David and Solomon the holy tabernacle was there ( 1Ki 3:4, 1Ki 3:5 9:3 ). Gent. n. גִּבְעֹנִי 2Sa 21:1, seq. [now prob. el-Jîb الجيب Rob. ii. 137].
